title: Method and system for determining a scaled respiratory flow rate and volume during respiration of a patient
abstract: The invention relates to a method for determining a scaled respiratory flow rate and volume during respiration of a patient, comprising the steps of: a) successively determining a plurality of values for a difference between a pressure in at least a first location in an upper respiratory tract of the patient and a reference pressure; b) deriving the scaled respiratory flow rate from the plurality of values for the pressure difference by using a first relationship; c) deriving the scaled respiratory volume from the plurality of values for the pressure difference by using a second relationship; and d) displaying the scaled respiratory flow rate derived in step b) and the scaled respiratory volume derived in step c) or a further variable derived from the scaled respiratory flow rate and volume in a graphical representation wherein step a) is performed during tidal breathing of the patient. The invention further relates to a system for performing this method.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A method for determining a scaled respiratory flow rate and volume during respiration of a patient, comprising the steps of: a) successively determining a plurality of values for a difference between a pressure in at least a first location in an upper respiratory tract of the patient and a reference pressure; b) deriving the scaled respiratory flow rate from the plurality of values for the pressure difference by using a first relationship; c) deriving the scaled respiratory volume from the plurality of values for the pressure difference by using a second relationship; and d) displaying the scaled respiratory flow rate derived in step b) and the scaled respiratory volume derived in step c) or a further variable derived from the scaled respiratory flow rate and volume in a graphical representation wherein step a) is performed during tidal breathing of the patient. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the scaled respiratory flow rate and scaled respiratory volume are displayed in a single graph. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the first and second relationships used in steps b) and c) are each generalized relationships which are substantially independent of the patient. 4. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein an obstacle is arranged in the upper respiratory tract before step a) is performed. 5. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first location in the upper respiratory tract is in a nostril of the patient and wherein the reference pressure is ambient pressure. 6. The method of claims 4 and 5, wherein the obstacle is arranged in the nostril between the first location and a nostril entry. 7. The method of claim 5 or 6, wherein there is a first location in each of the patientâs nostrils and wherein a first plurality of values for the pressure difference is determined in one of the patientâs nostrils and a second plurality of values for the pressure difference is determined in the other nostril. 8. The method of any one of claims 5-7, wherein the plurality of values is determined by calculation on the basis of direct measurement by a remote pressure sensor in fluid communication with the patientâs nostril(s). 9. The method of any one of claims 5-7, wherein the plurality of values is determined by direct measurement using a pressure sensor arranged in the patientâs nostril(s). 10. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein steps b) and c) are performed at a location that is remote from the patient, and wherein the plurality of values for the pressure difference is transmitted to the remote location. 11. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein step a) is performed for a plurality of breathing cycles during tidal breathing of the patient, and wherein derivation of the scaled respiratory flow rate in step b) and derivation of the scaled respiratory volume in step c) for each separate cycle include the use of the values for the pressure difference determined during the plurality of breathing cycles. 12. The method of any one of claims 3-11, wherein the first generalized relationship used in step b) to derive the scaled respiratory flow rate is: in which: and with K the number of breathing cycles taken into account, T the time needed to complete these breathing cycles, and in which Îp is the pressure difference and b is a constant which depends on the sign of Îp. 13. The method of any one of claims 3-12, wherein the second generalized relationship used in step c) to derive the scaled respiratory volume is: with K the number of cycles taken into account, and T the time needed to complete these breathing cycles. 14. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the derivations in steps b) and c) and/or the displaying in step d) are/is performed by a computer. 15. A system for determining a scaled respiratory flow rate and volume during respiration of a patient, comprising: a determination module configured for successively determining a plurality of values for a difference between a pressure in at least a first location in an upper respiratory tract of the patient and a reference pressure; a first derivation module configured for deriving the scaled respiratory flow rate from the plurality of values for the pressure difference by using a first relationship; a second derivation module configured for deriving the scaled respiratory volume from the plurality of values for the pressure difference by using a second relationship; and a display module for displaying the scaled respiratory flow rate and the scaled respiratory volume or a further variable derived from the scaled respiratory flow rate and volume in a graphical representation; wherein the determination module is configured to be operative during tidal breathing of the patient. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the display module is configured to display the scaled respiratory flow rate and scaled respiratory volume in a single graph. 17. The system of claim 15 or 16, wherein the first and second determination modules are each configured for using generalized relationships which are substantially independent of the patient. 18. The system of any one of claims 15-17, wherein the determination module comprises an obstacle configured to be arranged in the upper respiratory tract. 19. The system of any one of claims 15-18, wherein the determination module is configured for determining the pressure differences between a first location in a nostril of the patient and ambient pressure. 20. The system of claims 18 and 19, wherein the obstacle of the determination module is configured to be arranged in the nostril between the first location and a nostril entry. 21. The system of claim 19 or 20, wherein there is a first location in each of the patientâs nostrils and wherein the determination module is configured for determining a first plurality of values for the pressure difference in one of the patientâs nostrils and a second plurality of values for the pressure difference in the other nostril. 22. The system of any one of claims 19-21, wherein the determination module comprises a remote pressure sensor configured to be brought into fluid communication with the patientâs nostril(s) by a conduit having known characteristics. 23. The system of any one of claims 19-21, wherein the determination module comprises a pressure sensor configured to be arranged in the patientâs nostril(s). 24. The system of any one of claims 15-23, wherein the first and second derivation modules are at a location that is remote from the patient and are connected to the determination module for receiving the plurality of values for the pressure difference. 25. The system of any one of claims 15-24, wherein the determination module is configured for determining the plurality of values for the pressure difference for a plurality of breathing cycles during tidal breathing of the patient, and wherein the first and second derivation modules are each configured for using, for each separate cycle, the values for the pressure difference determined during the plurality of breathing cycles. 26. The system of any one of claims 17-25, wherein the first derivation module is configured to use the following relationship to derive the scaled respiratory flow rate: in which: and with K the number of breathing cycles taken into account, T the time needed to complete these breathing cycles, and in which Îp is the pressure difference and b is a constant which depends on the sign of Îp. 27. The system of any one of claims 17-26, wherein the second derivation module is configured to use the following relationship to derive the scaled respiratory volume: in which: and with K the number of breathing cycles taken into account, T the time needed to complete these breathing cycles, and in which Îp is the pressure difference and b is a constant which depends on the sign of Îp. 28. The system of any one of claims 15-27, wherein the first and second derivation modules and/or the display module form(s) part of a computer.
